title: Level 163
wikitext: {{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 163: “Blissful Emptiness”}} {{Audio|file=Aefcb28c90ed4d508319ad6b83ea1630.mp3|align=center|volume=0.1}} {{Headerbox|header=Have you finally found peace?|file=Windows wallpaper .jpg|speed=0}}{{Class 1_}} Level 163 is the 164th level of the Backrooms. It was discovered in July of 2007 and takes the appearance of grassy fields with rolling hills that stretch on for miles with no visible end in sight. The ground is entirely covered with lush grass that constantly shifts in the steady breeze.{{Background|file=Blissful emptiness .jpg}} == Description == {{Blurimage|file=4B538033-9BD0-4C4E-B5F5-FD4EFE6897C1.jpg|caption=The first known image of the level, circa 2009.}} {{Blurimage|file=87D32545-FE53-425B-A2DA-66E89B33403D.jpeg|caption=Another image showing a different location in Level 163.}} {{Blurimage|file=12E3AB6A-E442-4AF5-971A-B5AB7F68FB4A.jpeg|caption=The exterior of the old church.}} Based on the Great Plains region of the United States, Level 163 is a curiously empty and vast space devoid of nearly anything. It is also notable for its odd vibrancy and bizarre artificial atmosphere. Both time and climate are stagnate with the sun always set at high noon and the temperature never changing from 75F (24C). Sound works almost normally, aside from the slight abnormal echo and the relaxing ambient music overlapped with nostalgic summer sounds such as birds, cicadas and crickets omitting from an unknown source. A slight breeze carries the scent of freshly cut grass and cucumbers. Such perfect conditions make the level feel like a blissful utopia, and yet there's undeniably something artificial about its perfect nature. Despite your doubts and the acknowledgment that something feels off, you can’t help falling victim to the calming breeze and the cozy warmth of the sun. After all this world has distanced you far from the worries and struggles you faced earlier. Here you do not feel pain or sorrow and you never grow hungry or tired. The expansive emptiness fills you with a feeling of safety and solitude. One could walk for miles and never see a single tree or any other plant life. In fact, you could walk for miles and never see anything. This level is almost entirely devoid of any signs of life, human or otherwise. The only notable consistent anomalies that disrupt the eternal bliss are a singular road that continuously stretches along the length of the plains and occasional fences or power lines that dot the landscape. However according to accounts from some travelers and word-of-mouth rumors, there are manmade structures that can be found, but due to the sheer scale of this level, they can only be found by chance. ===The Old Church=== Believed to be the only building-like structure to exist within this level, there have been several reports of wanderers stumbling upon this old church. However, due to the fact this level is seemingly infinite, it is unknown whether it is one singular church or a bunch of identical ones spread out across the level. Regardless the church offers a nice resting place and a good landmark for traveling. Upon entry, wanderers report feeling uplifted and hopeful but also incredibly drowsy, as if being beckoned to rest for a while. There is a noticeable layer of dust in the air and it smells distinctly like your grandma’s house but the warmth of the light shining through the stained glass windows and the gentle cords of a distant organ seem to lull the wanderer to sleep. Upon waking up an unknown amount of time will have passed and because the sun is always in the center of the sky it is impossible to ever truly know how much, therefore it is advised against falling asleep to avoid getting disoriented. ===Wind Turbines=== These large industrial structures heavily contrast the organic environment of the rest of the level. There is a much lower chance of running into Wind Turbines while exploring level 163 as there are far fewer reports of travelers seeing them, but from the few pictures that exist, it is discernible that they are always found in large groups and on flat terrain. Oddly enough they are fully functional and theorized to be the source of all the wind on this level as there is a noticeable increase in wind speed upon getting closer to them. Wanderers report being unable to get within 150 feet of them due to the incredibly harsh winds. There has been discussion of using the Wind Turbines for energy harvesting but it is unknown what exactly powers them or how they are able to function. ===Farmsteads=== Farmsteads are abandoned rustic pioneer-style farms that are made up of nothing but fences, an old silo and a rickety windmill. They can be found fairly often but they seem to serve no purpose other than being a home for Cows?. There is a chance of finding almond water, cashew water, old hay, and a rusted sickle. There is not much to note about farmsteads other than the faint “ranch music” that only plays in their immediate vicinity.{{Audio|file=5849d3b033144b4e99a99d98ecc3bde1.mp3|align=center|volume=0.1}} ==The Night Time Event== {{Blurimage|file=F7E582FE-97FB-45BD-8662-7F6518FBEAB3.jpg|caption=A rare image of the “Night Time” event occurring in Level 163, taken on June 15, 2013.}} The Night Time Event is an anomalous phenomenon that occurs at random intervals. Despite the level being stuck permanently at mid-day, there is a low chance that it will suddenly change to the middle of the night. When this happens there is a notable loud clicking sound, like the pulling of a large lever or a light switch, and time will shift from mid-afternoon to midnight. The sun and bright blue sky will instantly be replaced with the full moon and stars and the previous summer day ambiance will grow completely silent only occasionally being interrupted by the sounds of owls and chirping frogs. Those who have experienced the Night Time Event have noted how the level feels far more empty than before and the sudden darkness leaves you with a feeling of intense loneliness. It is entirely unknown what triggers this event to occur as there is no consistent day-night cycle. A day can last anywhere from 24 hours to months on end while the night event will always last 8 hours. While it is a startling change in scenery, there is no threat caused by the sudden change to night and many have theorized that the level changing to night is its way of “powering off” in order to give itself a break while still maintaining the illusion that everything is natural and real.{{Audio|file=E5ca952dd8644b1e8fc289bcb55ee921.mp3|align=center|volume=0.1}} ==The Void Anomaly== Another important oddity to note is the occasional appearance of large black squares referred to as “voids”. They appear suddenly and have a very short life span with the longest recorded void lasting about 45 seconds. Voids seem to be imperfections or glitches within level 163 and if the level detects one it will terminate it instantly which is why they are so short lasting. Some have speculated that entering a void may take the wanderer to a different level or sub-level but they are unpredictable and likely dangerous so it is heavily encouraged to avoid them if one is spotted. ==Level 163 is Alive== While it’s still only a theory, it is heavily speculated that Level 163 itself is alive and is actively trying to disguise itself by artificially creating a perfect utopia. It is believed that Level 163 is some form of artificial intelligence that gained sentience and for whatever reason longs for human contact. To attract wanderers it has been slowly transforming itself into what it thinks humans see as perfection which is why it is always sunny with a gentle breeze and comfortable temperature. But the level doesn’t understand how life works and does not know that it needs to provide a water and food source for humans to live off of it. It is also unable to properly simulate life which is why it lacks so much vegetation and why there are no animals present aside from the “cows”. The sun and wind turbines are powered by the level itself and all sounds are produced by hidden speakers in an attempt to make the environment feel more real. It can not be said if Level 163’s attempts to draw in wanderers are done with good or bad intentions, but it has not made an effort to harm them, that being said it is still the backrooms and it is important to always be cautious. Remember that this is just a THEORY and there is no way to prove it is true unless the level itself wants us to know, we are entirely at its mercy. ==Entities == ===Cows?=== It’s hard to call them entities as they are more like objects or props. Their flat dimension and cartoonish appearance make them look very out of place and they can be found standing motionless in Farmsteads. They do not blink or breathe and offering them food does not affect them. However, if a wanderer leaves the area and comes back the cows will be in a different position than before but they will not move if you are within their eyesight. Additionally, interacting with the cows has a bizarre effect in which touching a cow will result in them no clipping to a different random level. Therefore it is advised to not interact with them to prevent cow-related injuries. ==Colonies/Outposts== While the conditions of the level are sustainable for life, the sheer size of the level prevents one from meeting other wanderers. ==Entrances and Exits== === Entrances === *Touching a cow that has been transported to a different level will result in being no clipped here. *Walking for an extended period of time through [[Level 55]] until reaching an area without any houses will lead to Level 163. *Falling asleep in “Grandma’s house” in [[Level 180]] has a small chance of the wanderer waking up in the Old Church. *Finding and exiting through an emergency exit door in [[Level 162]] usually leads to [[Level 338]]. However, some reports say that they can sometimes lead here too. ===Exits=== *Finding old hay or in rare cases a full haystack will take the wanderer to [[Level 10]]. *Climbing a curiously tall hill and attempting to touch the sky can occasionally transport you to [[Level 113]]. *Finding an out-of-place stable and entering it leads to [[Level 116]]. *Touching a wind turbine that appears to be broken will take you to [[Level 231]] but finding a broken wind turbine is incredibly unlikely. *Tripping over and noclipping during a Night Time event will lead to [[Level 164]].
